_comment: Spanish closing signals. Includes Colombian, Mexican, Argentine variants.

false_positive_guards._comment: Don't match 'listo para X' — user describing readiness, not closing
Don't match 'listo el X' — user announcing completion of subtask
Don't match 'ya está + más' — user wants to keep going
Don't match meta-questions about close signals
Don't match question-form prompts ending in '?'

_strict_guards_comment: Spanish counterparts of the en.json strict_guards tier (codified 2026-05-25). Required because false_positive_guards do NOT override an explicit/high_confidence match — see detect-closing-signal.py: 'Strong tiers (explicit, high_confidence) override FP guards'. Without this tier, every Spanish FP guard above is dead code against a high_confidence hit, and Spanish/Portuguese users had no way to suppress meta-discussion of the cascade.

strict_guards._comment: Meta-discussion / debugging of the close cascade itself ('por qué se cierra sola la sesión', 'arregla el regex de cierre de sesión', 'el hook de cierre dispara mal'). Requires a diagnostic/interrogative word + a close verb + a session/cascade noun, so a bare imperative close ('cierra esta sesión') is NOT suppressed.
User describing the bug pattern ('las sesiones se archivan solas', 'la sesión se cierra sola', 'las sesiones siguen disparando'). A genuine close never describes sessions in the third person.
Backtick/quote-wrapped technical references to close phrases (regex, code, or script content quoted in a question).
'cerrar la sesión de X' where X is a technical/other noun (base de datos, terminal, navegador, coaching…) — closing something else, not this conversation.

Meta-QUESTION about a close word — '¿qué significa chao?', '¿cómo se dice listo?'. Asking ABOUT a close phrase is referenced content, never a close itself. Strict because the weak-tier meta-question FP guard does NOT override a high_confidence match on the bare word.

INTERROGATIVE about whether a close ALREADY happened — '¿ya cerraste la sesión?', '¿la sesión quedó cerrada?'. A question about the close's state, not a request to perform it. Modal requests ('¿puedes cerrar la sesión?') carry no past/state verb, so they still fire.

INTERROGATIVE 'ya está/estuvo/fue/quedó + more content + ?' — the reported false positive: '¿Ya está en el prompt o no está creado?' fired the full cascade mid-explanation. Requires trailing content before the '?', so a bare '¿ya está?' is not caught here.
